item.title: The Star Of Saros Debunks A Theory I Wish He Hadn’t
item.content:encoded:
When I was playing Saros for review, I had a few conversations with other folks who were playing the game pre-release as we collectively tried to untangle the many dangling narrative threads of Housemarque’s latest roguelike shooter/psychological mystery. Though it’s not quite as abstract as Returnal, it does have a lot of mythology and character relationships that lend themselves to multiple and varied interpretations, and among the reviewers I spoke to, there was one prevailing theory that added layers to protagonist Arjun’s internal struggles. Now, however, actor Rahul Kohli has weighed in on this interpretation in a way that kinda makes me wish I could put the genie back in the bottle.

Your home base in Saros is the Passage, and it’s here that, between runs, Arjun can sometimes talk to his fellow surviving crew members of the Echelon IV expedition. Among these people, tucked away in a back corner of the Passage, is a man named Sebastian. From the way they speak to each other, it’s clear that Sebastian and Arjun have history back on Earth, having worked together in some fashion before our “hero” flew across space to find his wife Nitya on the time-distorting planet of Carcosa. Though they greet each other warmly, it soon becomes apparent in the conversations that follow that their relationship is strained for some reason, though neither of them get too specific, instead speaking in ominous, cutting lines about blame and guilt. Nevertheless, Sebastian stays at the base, tending to plants while Arjun fights monsters and the rest of the crew loses their minds under the influence of Carcosa’s sun.

Sebastian’s little nook on the Passage is next to a tree in which a portal periodically opens and, once entered, shows glimpses of Arjun’s past that gesture at some major happenings from before the events of Saros. In these visions, Arjun walks through a recreation of an alleyway back on Earth, one that sits between a night club he used to frequent, drinking his nights away, and a hotel where he seems to have been engaging in extramarital affairs with someone other than his wife. During one of the flashbacks, Arjun sees what looks like a dinner date setup, and as he examines the empty wine glasses sitting on the table, he mutters that whatever he was doing behind Nitya’s back “didn’t mean anything.” 

At first, it seems like we will never actually learn who Arjun cheated on Nitya with, but when I played the game, I started putting some clues together that made me think this mystery person had been right under my nose.

None of the other members of the Echelon crew interact with Sebastian, and Arjun never brings him up to anyone either. As more people on the team die or go missing, Arjun realizes that no one else has seen Sebastian, and there’s no record of him being part of the team sent from Earth to find out what happened to Nitya’s team. This former colleague is nothing but a hallucinated manifestation of Arjun’s inner guilt…but for what?

Flashbacks and text logs reveal to us that Sebastian died on Earth under mysterious circumstances, but Saros gestures at the truth as it repeatedly drags Arjun through his past. We go back to the hotel room he cheated in and see Sebastian appear, nonchalantly examining the window before it shatters, leaving Arjun muttering inconsolably when he comes to in the present day. The implications are strong, but we don’t hear Arjun admit to what has happened until much, much later.

In Saros’ final stretch, Arjun essentially goes on an apology tour, trying to make up to the people he’s done wrong or who were abandoned as he spent what turned out to be decades of real time chasing ghosts. One of his last stops is the portal where Sebastian once spent time tending to plants and tossing out snide remarks. Inside, we get confirmation that Arjun is a person of interest in Sebastian’s death, and find the latter hunched over against a wall next to the hotel, bleeding out.

With his last breaths, he asks Arjun one question: “What was I to you?”

For me, Arjun’s answer cleared the fog around so many of the game’s mysteries: “You were nothing..that’s what I told myself,” he says. “I didn’t deserve your trust.” The last time Arjun said something didn’t mean anything to him, he was looking at a dinner arrangement for whoever it was he was having an affair with. The understanding that Sebastian had been Arjun’s secret lover, and that he killed him to keep up appearances and cover up his lies to Nitya about late nights out with coworkers, hit me like a wrecking ball.

I was frankly floored by how much Saros clicked into place for me when I came to this conclusion. Saros spends its flashbacks painting Arjun as an absolute bastard, one whose worldview was shaped by an abusive father who raised him on a foundation of sacrosanct masculine ideals that led him to lash out at his wife and himself, a byproduct, in my view, of the internalized homophobia he felt as he kept his relationship with Sebastian secret. My read on Saros was by no means a happy gay love story, but the older I get, the more I appreciate queer stories that underline that we are just as broken and capable of doing wrong as anyone else. It added so much texture to Arjun as a character and to Saros as a story, it became pretty central to what made the game’s failed road to redemption work for me. 

This was the theory myself and numerous other reviewers I spoke to all came away with, and yet, when the game launched, I only saw it coming up sparingly in the public eye. Saros isn’t quite as obtuse as Returnal, but it is still a story built upon interpretation, so I figured that as long as some people had this reading, it wasn’t entirely out of left field. Now, however, Arjun’s actor Kohli is on the post-launch interview circuit, and has put a dent in the whole thing.

In an interview with Reforge Gaming, Kohli talks about how the “what was I to you?” line has been interpreted, and says that Housemarque never gave him or Sebastian actor David DeSantos any direction that the two’s relationship was meant to be romantic.

There’s the line again that I saw which is being kind of interpreted in different ways, which is when Sebastian asks Arjun, “What was I to you?” And he said, “you were nothing. That’s what I told myself.” People are looking into romantic things about that. When we played it on set, the idea was what was I to you in terms of my friendship? What was my death to you? Yeah. How were you able to compartmentalize what you did and then move on? How were you able to have blood on your hands and tell me that we were, tell me that you cared about me, that we were boys or whatever and do it. And Arjun’s way of handling it was I told myself you [were] nothing. It is what it is. Casualty of war. So that’s what our intentions [were]. I saw some people read into Sebastian and Arjun being in a relationship. Um, yeah? If you think so? I mean it’s up to you. That’s not what I played, you know. That’s not what me and David were playing. I do think if we were playing with an idea that Arjun and Sebastian had hooked up, I think the Housemarque [folks] would have told the actors that there was more to their relationship than something platonic. IIt wouldn’t help us to not let us into that. So, I was never told that. Neither was David who played Sebastian.

To be clear, Kohli’s not done anything wrong by talking about this. Actors and developers talk about their projects, the process of making them and the intention behind them,  every day. However, Saros, which uses ambiguity and abstraction to its advantage, is by design a story that lends itself to interpretation. This is the kind of story that generates hours-long video essays breaking down mythology, obscure references, data logs, and subtle pieces of dialogue to illustrate a broader thesis about what it all means. The dissection is just as much a part of the experience as the shooting and swerving through brightly colored bullets. 

Saros isn’t the only story that’s making me think about this issue right now. Recently, Obsession director Curry Barker has been giving interviews about his (incredible) romantic horror movie, and has also been more or less “confirming” parts of the story that were once up to interpretation, and while I still love the film, I feel like the clarity these responses have provided has, paradoxically, only served to muddy my understanding of the film. Parts of it now make less sense to me than they once did. 

The trouble is, as much as there’s an audience for abstract storytelling, there’s just as much of an audience for AI-voiced explainer videos, lore breakdowns, and Wikipedia trivia bullet points that break everything down into a perceived “objective” truth. Typically, this is a futile effort, as anything you put out into the world is up to the interpretation of the audience, but a lot of that magic and debate goes away when you have creatives outright telling fans what the intention was, and it’s especially hard to talk about abstract works without stepping on interpretations fans have latched onto; now, when Saros fans who see Arjun as straight debate its story, they’ll have ammunition against the SebastianxArjun truthers, as if queer readings don’t already face enough scrutiny when it’s plausible that the creators of a work intended for it to be read that way. Saros has some explicitly queer characters and relationships in its supporting cast and lore, but it felt pretty bold for a male main character in a AAA game to potentially be queer, even in a story as tragic as this.

So now, what am I left with? Saros is still one of my favorite games of the year, and I can still hold onto the view that Arjun is a narcissistic bisexual bastard that traveled across the galaxy for a humility lesson. But my admiration for the swings the game takes doesn’t feel quite as strong as it did when I could believe that might have been the intention. Despite my job of talking to creatives about the games they make, I tend to take the most from fiction when it’s able to speak for itself. My favorite games are often the ones I’ve never heard the creators say a word about. It’s a hard line to walk because I love hearing someone passionately explain the ideas or inspirations or experiences that led to the creation of something, but more than that, I love to see what introspection their work brings out in me, whether they intended it or not. Saros is the kind of story I like best when its meanings are left entirely to my own imagination, and today the battle between creator intention and player interpretation is often fought in black-and-white absolutes. I miss when I thought I saw more colors; now I have to squint and look at Saros from a different angle just to make sure they were there in the first place.



https://kotaku.com/saros-theory-arjun-sebastian-romance-affair-cheating-2000700260

item.title: Witcher 3 Devs Tease Expansion Size, More Gwent, And A Mysterious New Sword After Confirming It Was Originally Supposed To Come Out This Year
item.content:encoded:
This week, CD Projekt Red officially announced a new expansion for The Witcher 3 called Songs of the Past. While it’s being stingy with concrete details beyond a 2027 release window, we did get a few more notable teases during today’s anniversary livestream. The meager morsels provide a decent sense of what to expect from the new expansion and enough intrigue for fan speculation to run rampant for months.

The first question on everyone’s mind is just how big should players expect Songs of the Past to be? The Witcher 3 came out over a decade ago. Could anyone really expect the open-world RPG to get another sprawling update, especially when the internal development team (the studio is collaborating with Fool’s Theory) is in the midst of trying to ship The Witcher 4?

While CDPR has repeatedly signaled that it doesn’t do expansions that are small, staff on the livestream made a more concrete commitment. “Songs of the Past will be aligned with what you’re familiar with in Blood and Wine, and what you’ve come to experience and expect from us when we do our expansions,” said social media manager Laura Beitzel.

For anyone who forgets or never played it, Blood and Wine was longer than many whole games. The roughly 15-hour main campaign took Geralt to an entirely new map and set him about trying to solve a series of murders involving vampires and no shortage of palace intrigue. With a completionist run lasting close to 30 hours, it was even more sprawling than Cyberpunk 2077‘s Phantom Liberty expansion.

The team also confirmed that Songs of the Past will expand The Witcher 3‘s hit minigame Gwent with fresh cards. Blood and Wine also had an entire new Gwent tournament. While I don’t want to get greedy, it would be no small treat to get another one in Songs of the Past.

And then there’s what the expansion will actually be about. We know that The Witcher 4 jumps into the future and switches the narrative focus from Geralt to Ciri. Amid rumors that Songs of the Past will attempt to bridge the space between The Witcher 3 and its sequel, there’s been speculation that it will revolve in part around how Ciri gets her new sword.

Geralt is carrying three in the expansion’s first promotional image, leading to a theory that the new blade will eventually be the one Ciri is seen with in the current Witcher 4 trailer footage. “It is a very important sword for the story and you will get to know it when the game comes out,” community manager Amelia Korzycka said. “So a few more streams before that when [we] reveal it.”

Beitzel gave a more direct clue: “There are some hints waiting for you on a very important evening in Witcher lore, Belleteyn Night, in our Belleteyn celebration that we shared a few weeks ago.” That’s a reference to a special festival, concept art for which CDPR shared earlier this month. It shows Geralt sitting on the ground near a fire while the bard Dandelion strums on his mandolin.

“Tonight, there are no monsters to kill, no roads to roam, Just quiet thoughts and wishes that try to find a home,” read an accompanying caption. “The White Wolf sits still, mesmerized by the dancing flame, While Dandelion strums his lute and hums Geralt’s name.”

Ciri is believed to have been born around the time of the festival, which is supposed to take place around May. While fans can dissect what the rest of this might mean for the plot of Songs of the Past, it at least now seems to strongly suggest we’ll finally get to play a brand-new Witcher 3 expansion in the first half of 2027.

The only bad news is that’s not when the expansion was originally supposed to come out. CDPR confirmed on an earnings call today that it had originally hoped to ship it this year. “We had a moment where our plans for Songs of the Past, [it] would be released this year,” CD Projekt Red joint CEO Michał Nowakowski said. “However we decided, together with the development team, that the game will be launching in 2027 to achieve the best possible result from the consumer standpoint, which in the end, frankly speaking, is the only thing that really matters.”



https://kotaku.com/witcher-3-devs-tease-expansion-size-more-gwent-and-a-mysterious-new-sword-after-confirming-it-was-originally-supposed-to-come-out-this-year-2000700250

item.title: Insider Gaming Showcase—Everything Announced
item.content:encoded:
After months of work behind the scenes, the debut Insider Gaming Showcase premiered on Thursday, May 28. The show featured 55 games, including over a dozen world premieres.

You saw games like the reveals of Toypunk and Bassai-Dai, along with new trailers from Unyverse and Combo Devils. But we know not everyone is able to watch a full show or follow everything at once. That’s why we have put together the complete rundown of every game that was shown during the Insider Gaming Showcase.

Check out all of the games, wishlist the ones you like, and let us know what stood out to you down in the comments and over in the Insider Gaming Discord.

Every Game At The Insider Gaming Showcase

Hawthorn – Near Studios | Full Coverage

Former developers from Bethesda, BioWare, and Naughty Dog bring you a sandbox RPG realm of anthropomorphic animals and fairy creatures. Play co-op or solo as you build a village of memorable NPCs, farm, craft, explore, and celebrate the seasons in an ever-changing world bigger than you.

Where the Chips Fall – Coquito Games | Full Coverage

Balatro Meets Blackjack with a Cuphead aesthetic in this casino rogue-lite where you gamble with your abilities. Every chip you bet can turn the tide in your favor, but you could also lose it all in a bad hand. Think you can beat the house?

Hexrite – Phabula | Full Coverage

You are a sorcerer seeking to fulfil a magical ritual to summon an ancient dead deity. Cast mystical domes, explore fantastical realms and meet a peculiar cast of characters in this stylized PS2-inspired action-platformer!

Zoominoes – Starlight Games inc. | Full Coverage

Build the ultimate Zoo in this number-go-up roguelike deckbuilder. Find powerful mythical animals, boost them with snacks, and even manipulate the board itself. Break the game by scoring millions (and more) with insane combos.

ZeroSpace – Starlance Studios | Full Coverage

ZeroSpace is a sci-fi hybrid strategy game with a story-rich, party-based RPG campaign. As commander of the Protectorate forces, you must upgrade your mothership, build strong relationships with your crew and recruit a formidable army. Navigate the hard choices that will shape humanity’s future.

Toypunk – Eighth Lantern | Full Coverage

A comedic third-person shooter roguelite where you control customizable puppets armed with an infinite toybox of absurd weaponry. Fight hordes of cotton-stuffed baddies across fantastical biomes with your friends or on your own. Experience crazy synergies and unlock more toys between every run!

End of Ember – Bad Plan Studios | Full Coverage

Explore a gory Hell in this twin-stick shooter that pays homage to badass horror franchises. Kill demons, upgrade items, and fight weird and gross bosses until they explode in piles of gore. Who is Ember? What is reality?

Eclipse 2: Invasion of Darkness – MadRock | Full Coverage

Eclipse 2: Invasion of Darkness – is an isometric, story-driven game in the Action RPG genre, combining the mechanics of metroidvania exploration and the dynamics of modern slashers. Lead the medieval resistance against the alien invasion on Earth!

Ungaland – MevStudio | Full Coverage

“Dive into a prehistoric world of adventure where the power of primal seeds changes everything. Unlock abilities, attach them to your weapons, and face challenges in a vibrant land full of action, and surprises.”

Monster Mercs – Izak Games | Full Coverage

Transform into the monsters you slay and unleash new spells and abilities. Team up with up to 3 friends, take on unique contracts to get paid, upgrade your character and gear, and unlock powerful transformations in this fast-paced co-op action RPG.

The Stinky Adventures of DungDung – Rexroth Studios | Full Coverage

The Stinky Adventures of DungDung is a love-letter to many of the classic platformers we’ve played over the years. We draw inspiration from the likes of Jak & Daxter, Ratchet & Clank, and Sly Cooper. But we also asked ourselves, “What if we could mess with the world in small ways, inspired by greats such as Legend of Zelda: Tears of the Kingdom? (Or when we’d just enter console cheat codes.)

Project Eclipse – Unlikely Heroes Studio | Full Coverage

Meowstery Wisp – DMT Games | Full Coverage

A multiplayer social deduction and investigation game where players gather clues, debate, and manipulate trust to resolve a mystery; with no elimination until the big finale.

Lunar Strike – Cognition Europe | Full Coverage

Lunar Strike blends speculative fiction with a game world grounded in hard science. The game focuses on compelling survival and character-driven storytelling over combat. A race against time to preserve the evidence of humanity’s presence on the Moon before its inevitable collapse and destruction.

Unyverse – Afrime Studios | Full Coverage

Unyverse is an Afrofuturistic RPG With Fighting Game Mechanics Where Your Fists, Choices, and Inner Power Change the Fate of a Galaxy.

Good News – Augusto Salgado | Full Coverage

You’ve been hired as a chief editor to make this newspaper grow, but your writers are awful. Correct their drafts, set the tone of the news to fit your audience, make readership grow; and form a relationship with the shady politicians that will push you to give them good press in an electoral year

Carrier Cuties – Cornerstone Interactive Studios | Full Coverage

Carrier Cuties fast-paced 3D arena fighting game where Pets in Hu-Mechs battle in highly interactive and destructible environments using the surroundings to fight. Battle with a partner against two opponents to win all the glory and treats!

Terminal War – Albatross Interactive | Full Coverage

Terminal War is a grounded, team-based third-person shooter set in a fractured America during the late 1990s. Engage in intense combat across war-torn cities, forgotten towns, and contested territory where every bullet, every strike, and every decision counts.

Ward 107: Silence – Gaetano Tonzus | Full Coverage

WARD 107: SILENCE is a first person psychological horror. Your microphone is the key: use your voice to solve vocal puzzles and uncover the atrocious experiments of Project H.O.P.E. Speak to progress, but silence is your only defense when they hunt you. Make noise, and you die.

Alea – Afterlife Games | Full Coverage

Intense, fast-paced arena battles with a high skill ceiling. Alea is a skill-first hero brawler with quick, high-pressure matches and a broad array of characters. Master two sides of every hero and forge a playstyle that is uniquely your own to outplay your rivals in 1v1, 2v2 and 3v3 matches.

Take That Mainstage! – Fingers on the Moon | Full Coverage

It’s 1992 in Emerald City. The world screams for change. You made the leap, now fulfill your destiny and change the world. Build your band, write real music you perform, manage relationships, and reach the top of the charts. In this hybrid life sim, be the one to Take That Mainstage!

Brimvahl Crown – Chubby Puppy Pte.Ltd | Full Coverage

Brimvahl Crown is a narrative driven action RPG that follows the story of princess Kazmiel, a legendary seraph that was exiled from her kingdom. Now that the dark rises, Kazmiel must return to action. Dodge, Parry, and strike enemies down and save the world from the might of the Deathless King!

Mithra – Seventh Disorder | Full Coverage

Investigate the world’s first cult in a cryptic mystery adventure that blurs the lines between fiction and reality. We’ve been keeping an eye on you. Follow us further down the rabbit hole…

Cursed Blood – DM Studios | Full Coverage

Cursed Blood is a brutal 1-4 player co-op melee roguelike. Play as vengeful Samurai Apes fueled by rage, unleashing a whirlwind of lightning-fast combat. Spill blood, sever limbs, and claim your vengeance!

DekaDuck – Asteristic Game Studio | Full Coverage

DekaDuck is a 2D action platformer, featuring fully hand-drawn art and animation. Fight enemies using their own abilities and quirks to solve puzzles and defeat bosses, in a sci-fi world full of conspiracies!

Hounded – IMPRNT STUDIOS | Full Coverage

You are Duke, a determined young Border Collie who is searching for their lost master, the leader of The Rebellion. Hounded by a cunning hunter, can you track the scent, unearth the power of The Tide and find your way home?

Dicetris – Leik Games | Full Coverage

A puzzle game where numbers matter. Dicetris fuses classic block-stacking with a fresh numerical twist: build columns of coloured dice and match the exact sum at the base to clear them. Use sharp maths and smarter strategy to stay ahead as you plan, stack, and clear your way through each challenge.

My Pet Hooligan – AMGI Studios | Full Coverage

Liberate your city from Zuck Corp in this 1-4 player co-op TPS with roguelite elements. Choose your arsenal of Gadgets, Buffs and cartoon weapons. Blast bots and skateboard as you regain your turf. Get stronger each zone on your way to beat the evil overlord MetaZuckBot! Ready to join the Rebellion?

Cubex – Gamez Studio | Full Coverage

Cubex is a horror escape room game in first person, collaborative (or not) for up to 4 players, where your objective will be to find the exit of a giant cube.

Smash ‘N Grab – SLAP-BANG! Digital | Full Coverage

Smash ‘N Grab is a destructive, speedrunning beat ’em up starring Smash and Grab – a robot and raccoon duo pulling off explosive heists. Tethered by a rope, they race against the clock for the perfect score!

Hope in the City – Lofty Sky Entertainment | Full Coverage

17-year-old Hope Song returns home from school to find her parents missing. Use Hope’s observant eye as an artist to investigate, piece together clues, and uncover dark secrets beneath her peaceful urban life in this non-linear narrative mystery game.

AZUR – KADES Studio | Full Coverage

Azur is a fast-paced PvP survival FPS set on the French island of Belmas. Every time you respawn, complete missions, explore, and survive as long as possible to secure the best gear, with the risk of losing everything.

Rollergirl – Pushing Vertices | Full Coverage

Choose your own vibe in this slice-of-life adventure. While exploring a small town on rollerblades, use your MP3 player to set the mood for your summer. Run errands for quirky neighbours, get entangled in local drama and stumble into a teenage romance.

Brave New Wonders – City From Naught Inc. | Full Coverage

Command a tribe of intelligent automatons that can interpret ANY customized instruction you give in Brave New Wonders, a factory automation adventure. Pioneer a post-apocalyptic world, explore ruins from the past & discover a mysterious reality as you make your automated factories thrive!

Malice In Wonderland – Slyglass Games | Full Coverage

Tear through Wonderland and break free of The Hatter’s control in this fast-paced action roguelike. Augment your abilities to destroy your foes and escape before you’re out of time!

Paddler – Kosma Co. | Full Coverage

You’re the bartender at a lazy river pool bar. Type ingredients in rhythm to mix drinks and serve the rush. A typing game for enthusiasts who want their keystrokes to matter.

Project Mourning Reign – IronRoot Games | Full Coverage

Island of Winds – Parity Games | Full Coverage

Island of Winds is an adventure game set in a fantastical world inspired by 17th century Iceland and folklore. Experience the story of Brynhildur, a Balance Keeper who embarks on a journey of self-discovery. Core gameplay involves intriguing puzzles, spellcasting, and a focus on empathy encounters.

AGX GP – TYP1 Games | Full Coverage

AGX is a heart thumpingly fast, Anti-Gravity racing game. It’s lightning‑fast action and Xtreme ability‑based gameplay, will push your reactions and skills to the limit! Welcome To AGX!

GRIMPS – Watt Studio | Full Coverage

A dark, gritty action shooter featuring savage plushie aliens. Tear the Grimps to shreds, burn ’em down, or blast ’em with the Shrimpozooka—the Plushpocalypse is here!

Bassai-Dai – Kaloplay | Full Coverage

BASSAI-DAI is a stylized action-adventure game that blends fast-paced combat
with creative, ink brush-based gameplay.

Etre – Studio Lifeforms | Full Coverage

Enter an ancient alien mind spiraling toward collapse. Explore shifting inner worlds, unlock new abilities, and battle hostile anomalies in a 3D third-person action adventure. Stabilize the mind to forge a new world or let it awaken as something far more terrifying.

Crete – Mayhem Mirror Studios | Full Coverage

Fight against the Ancestors and other biopunk creatures. Use their DNA to build your own procedural weapons, find a friend and combine your skills to be even more powerful. The further you go, the better rewards you will get for the next run. It is up to you to reveal what is up there in the north.

Becoming Pablo – Tentworks Interactive | Full Coverage

Rule the underworld in this test of grand-strategy and city-building set in the 70’s-90’s drug trade. Conquer new territories, manage operations, and outsmart your rivals. Becoming Pablo isn’t just about building an empire – it’s about surviving it.

Project Madras – Royston Cinemo | Full Coverage

Project Madras – An open world Indie game setup in Chennai aka Madras. The city is the canvas and an exciting gaming experience is taking shape just for you makkale!

Combo Devils – Punkzilla | Full Coverage

Combo Devils is an evolution of the platform-fighting genre. Stylish combat, and a cooperative extraction roguelite mode offers content for all player skill levels. An experience that balances accessibility with a high execution ceiling for a dedicated core player base to grow around.

Rusthaven – Chaos Theory Games | Full Coverage

Rusthaven is a cosy management game about rebooting the world in the anti-apocalypse. Journey across an abandoned planet on a solar skyship, salvaging broken-down bots in need of rescue. Clean up the world, upcycle retro technology, and build a peaceful sanctuary where all can thrive and belong.

Valorborn – Laps Games | Full Coverage

Dive into a living medieval fantasy sandbox RPG where the world moves with or without you and factions rise and fall. Become a thief, hunter, or sellsword, survive alone or with a party, and shape your story in a fully enterable world where every building, cave, and road hides opportunity or danger.

Blind Descent – Pokuch | Full Coverage

Blind Descent is an open-world survival adventure that unveils the mysteries of the Martian depths. Play solo or with friends in 4-player co-op as you search for the missing team. Adapt to survive by building custom tools, upgrading the base, and/or mutating in this bizarre alien world.

Don’t Lose Aggro – Oren Koren | Full Coverage

Protect your team by forcing enemies to attack you in this single-player action roguelite inspired by MMO raids. Create unique builds, unlock powerful talents and master the art of tanking to command the chaos!

Waters of Ragnarök – Polar Region Games | Full Coverage

Waters of Ragnarök is a tactical naval survival game set after the Norse apocalypse. Navigate cursed seas, explore the lands, scavenge the massive corpses of Jötunns, and lead your crew through tactical battles against survivors and monsters in this Norse survival exploration game.

Everything is Gun! – Incineration Productions | Full Coverage

Developed by Incineration Productions for PC and consoles, Everything Is Gun! rejects modern cinematic hand-holding in favor of raw mechanical depth and unapologetic, high-stakes speed.

Deep Dive – Sky Lantern Studios | Full Coverage

Eggstinct – Manaforce Studios | Full Coverage

Eggstinct! is a cooperative, physics-driven dinosaur adventure where you and your friends must work together to rescue fragile eggs and survive chaotic prehistoric hazards.

Trickshotterz – Lumari Games | Full Coverage

BEER PONG GETS GUNS… Only shots in the top count, pick abilities, grab wild powerups, and pull off absurd trickshots that hit harder the crazier they are. Play with up to 4 players locally, online, or hop into public matchmaking.

Mike has been covering the gaming industry since 2012, and has reported on some of the largest events in the industry while also working as an investigative reporter. Outside of…

More from Mike Straw

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android

item.title: Nintendo’s newest WarioWare is a weirdo smartphone app
item.content:encoded:
A decade ago, Nintendo made a big splash into the world of mobile gaming with a new Super Mario platformer directed by none other than Shigeru Miyamoto. But even though the game proved popular, it wasn’t the success the company had hoped for. Over the ensuing years Nintendo has slowly retreated from smartphone gaming, with the exception of a handful of apps and some legacy games. Which is what made it so surprising this week when Nintendo launched Pictonico. It’s a bizarre and playful mobile game that channels the best of WarioWare, but I have zero idea how it fits into Nintendo’s current strategy.

Much like WarioWare, Pictonico (which, I admit, I’m not entirely sure how to pronounce) is a collection of microgames that last just a few seconds each. In each round, you play 10 of these in quick succession, and usually you have just enough time to figure out what you actually need to do before moving on to the next thing. You’re given a simple command like, say, “chomp,” and then you have to do something like grab hold of a mouth and make it chew some food. The games are all very silly in often hilarious ways, so you’ll be plucking hair, licking lollipops, and peeling bananas as quickly as you can.

The twist in Pictonico is that the games all use photos on your camera roll to customize the experience. The game pulls faces from photos and slips them into the microgames, so I found myself making my wife chomp down on kebab with a disturbingly large mouth, or rubbing a lamp to see a buff genie version of my 10-year-old pop out. As an example, here is me as a ballerina waiting to get their photo taken:

The game lets you choose which photos you want to appear in the game so things don’t get uncomfortably weird, and it does occasionally pull things that aren’t human faces. At one point I had to match up an image that had been broken up into three parts, and it was a photo I had taken during my time reporting on the Nintendo Museum in Kyoto. Pictonico is a free download on both iOS and Android, but that only gets you access to a demo version; to play the full thing, which includes 80 different minigames, you have to buy two content packs priced at $7.69 and $5.99 each.

Despite its strange name and lack of familiar characters, Pictonico is an extremely Nintendo production. Not only does it play like WarioWare, but its offbeat humor reminds me a lot of the Tomodachi Life games. (It also feels spiritually connected to the now-defunct Miitomo, Nintendo’s first ever mobile app.) Which is all to say that it’s fun, charming, and almost ideally designed for its particular platform. But it’s also one of those moments — like Labo and Alarmo before it — where I just have to throw up my hands and admit that it can be impossible to predict what this company will do. In many of the biggest and most important ways Nintendo plays it safe. But then it releases something like Pictonico and you’re reminded that it’s at its very best when it stays weird.

Follow topics and authors from this story to see more like this in your personalized homepage feed and to receive email updates.

  • Andrew Webster


https://www.theverge.com/games/939283/pictonico-nintendo-mobile-game

item.title: Red Frame is a Witnessian puzzle game where every red frame you find has a conundrum for you to solve
item.content:encoded:
There are plenty of philosophical critiques to be had of The Witness (and its creator at large), but there was something still quite appealing about the idea of a world harbouring puzzles in unexpected places. Thankfully, there's a new one of those on the way that can knock The Witness off of its podium called Red Frame, a time manipulation game set in a buried mansion where if you can see something that might look like a red frame, you're certain to find a puzzle.

This very thinky game was shown off thanks to the Thinky Direct (important disclosure: former reviews editor Rachel Watts co-hosted the presentation), and showed off plenty of puzzles that look like they require some mighty thinkering. Upon being met with a red frame, your tasks will vary. They start simple, like simply moving a gate out the way, an action that can only be done through the window of the frame. These then undulate in form; perhaps you need to play with physics to get a marble to roll in just the right way, or on another occasion you need to help a bed of flowers grow along a particular path.

The red frames differ from locale to locale too, occasionally being no more than that, a red pictureframe, other times a touch subtler in that they are barriers of a playbox (though there is only so subtle you can make the colour red). I do hope that these too will require some clever sleuthing to uncover, as there's nothing more satisfying than solving a secret puzzle.

In total there are apparently over 100 puzzles to solve, and you're able to "manipulate time to replay scenarios, identify cause and effect, and reveal new insights." I'm not entirely sure how that works just yet as it isn't clear whether it's been shown off or not, but it's there all the same. Some amount of story appears to be lurking within as well, as the aforementioned mansion is home to some family's "historical legacy." I suppose that's for us to uncover! Just not yet, as there's no release date currently. You can wishlist it on Steam, at least.



https://www.rockpapershotgun.com/red-frame-is-a-witnessian-puzzle-game-where-every-red-frame-you-find-has-a-conundrum-for-you-to-solve

item.title: Bloodborne Sicko Who Spent 11 Years Helping People With One Boss Is Retiring Because Nobody Is Playing It Anymore
item.content:encoded:
For one Bloodborne fan, it is the end of an era. After helping thousands of other players across over a decade beat one specific and tricky boss in FromSoftware’s beloved PlayStation 4 exclusive, Noahman is finally retiring because he just doesn’t get summoned anymore. He’s even stopped paying for PlayStation Plus as a result and is now determined to move on with his life.

On May 27, Noahman posted on the FromSoftware subreddit that it was “time to say goodbye to Bloodborne.” More specifically, Noahman was saying goodbye to The Orphan of Kos, a boss that he had been fighting over and over again for over a decade.

“Thousands of fights, Thousands of kills,” posted the dedicated Bloodborne fan. “But the playerbase has shrunk to a point where I don’t get summoned anymore. If you ever summoned me, thank you.”

Gamesradar chatted with Noahman after he announced his retirement from the game. He explained the reason he focused all his Bloodborne energy on Kos, saying that the boss is “easily the highest demand boss in the game for summon requests.” So it is the fastest way to get back into the action. After playing the fight over and over and helping people out, he “started to really like the boss even more than I already did. Fighting the Orphan was so, so much [fun]. I started to get good, and I liked the feeling of helping people out.”

Interestingly, Noahman tells the outlet that this was his first FromSoftware game.

For Noahman, who mostly played on Fridays and weekends, multiplayer summoning in Bloodborne was the perfect way to interact with other people. “The relationship between Summoner and Cooperator is like two ships passing in the night. Except in this instance, I’d help the ship get to port safely and be on my way.” The dedicated player tells the outlet he has helped anywhere between 5,000 to 6,000 hunters defeat Kos over the last decade or so.

As for why he stopped, Noahman explained in a follow-up post on Reddit that he was only paying for PlayStation Plus to help people defeat this one boss in Bloodborne. And now that he rarely gets summoned, he can’t “justify” paying for it anymore. “I’m basically paying to sit in a cave and not get summoned,” said Noahman. Maybe he’ll return for that remake that is totally happening one day…probably. Maybe.



https://kotaku.com/bloodborne-sicko-who-spent-11-years-helping-people-with-one-boss-is-retiring-because-nobody-is-playing-it-anymore-2000700265

item.title: Lunar Strike Gets New Gameplay Trailer at Insider Gaming Showcase
item.content:encoded:
Lunar Strike, a stunning single-player sci-fi title grounded in realism, has just received a new gameplay trailer during the Insider Gaming Showcase. Lunar Strike is an all-new exploration-driven, narrative-led title from Cognition, and it’s the team’s first game as a collective.

While Lunar Strike doesn’t have a release window, you’ll want to add this one to your wishlist if you’re a fan of all things sci-fi. This might be a fictional title, but it’s highly speculative and, in the words of the team, is ‘grounded in hard science.’

Needless to say, the sheer base concept of the game has me intrigued.

What is Lunar Strike?

Lunar Strike is an exploration-focused game set on Earth’s moon around 100 years in the future. Players assume control of an archivist sent to document the fall of humanity’s presence on the rocky landscape, but as you take off to handle that mission, catastrophe strikes.

Players are forced to contend with hazardous conditions, all rooted in real science, while choosing how and what to document as the world around them crumbles.

There’s a branching narrative to explore, and your choices shape the game before you.

Key Features

Logic, Not Violence

Lunar Strike focuses on telling a story while surviving against the atmosphere and not enemies. That said, you need to focus on using a range of archival scanning tools and fighting against the elements rather than some kind of alien lifeform. Depleting oxygen, suit energy, and habitat systems all come into question.

Forensic Reconstruction

Use your high-tech tools of the future to reconstruct scenes and solve mysteries of what happened to bring about the untimely end of the human race on the Moon.

Real Scientific Principles

Lunar Strike is grounded in real science, which means everything from orbital dynamics, survival systems, and the environment you explore is modeled on current lunar research and knowledge.

Lunar Strike Developer Interview

Check out our interview with Cognition:

The Studio Behind Lunar Strike

Cognition Europe is an independent studio based in Belgium and nurturing a passion for telling narrative-driven stories alongside a love for space science and speculative exploration. The studio was opened in 2022 and is an affiliate of Cognition US

The studio has ties to a years-old non-profit organization with a mission to promote ‘the digital conservation and protection of humanity’s collective culture and history.’

Links and More Information

  • Website: https://cognition.eu.com/
  • Store: https://store.steampowered.com/app/3949580/Lunar_Strike/

Does Lunar Strike look like your kind of game? Let us know on the Insider Gaming Discord server

Senior Editor and Esports Lead

Grant has been gaming for 30+ years and in the industry for 10+. You'll probably find him playing a post-apocalyptic game or an extraction shooter somewhere.

item.title: Intel makes a bid for handheld gaming PCs with new Arc G3 processors
item.content:encoded:
Most of the Steam Deck imitators on the market right now use AMD silicon, specifically the Ryzen Z-series chips. These are the same chips AMD makes for regular laptops, but with different power settings better suited to a compact handheld system. There are handhelds based on Intel silicon (MSI’s Claw is the main one), but Intel hasn’t yet tried making silicon marketed specifically for that purpose.

Today, the company is throwing its hat in the ring with two Intel Arc G-series processors, which will allow gaming handhelds to leverage the company’s genuinely quite good Arc B-series integrated GPUs. Intel says that several Arc G-series handhelds will arrive “starting in June 2026, with broader availability throughout the year.” These systems will include a new MSI Claw model, a Predator Atlas 8 from Acer, and a device from OneXPlayer.

Intel normally uses its “Arc” branding for integrated and dedicated GPUs, but in this case, the “Arc” brand encompasses the entire chip, including the CPU, GPU, NPU, and other components.

The G-series chips are similar in many ways to the Core Ultra Series 3 chips (codenamed Panther Lake) that Intel is currently shipping in high-end thin-and-light laptops. They use the same CPU and GPU architectures and make use of Intel’s 18A manufacturing process (among others). But they ship with a slightly different combination of CPU and GPU cores that doesn’t quite match up with any of the Core Ultra processors.



https://arstechnica.com/gadgets/2026/05/intel-makes-a-bid-for-handheld-gaming-pcs-with-new-arc-g3-processors/

item.title: Hawthorn’s Latest Trailer Revealed at Insider Gaming Showcase
item.content:encoded:
Hawthorn is a very special game. Former developers from Bethesda, BioWare, and Naughty Dog have teamed up to bring players a Sandbox RPG that supports co-op or solo experiences as they build their villages. The game also has personality-rich NPCs. The game is set in Windermere Valley, and while the inhabitants are all concerned about the reasons The Village was abandoned, the players have the opportunity to grow their own communities and create a fresh start for everyone. As this is a life simulator, players have the chance to engage with all of the creatures in whatever way they choose. You can be nice, or you can offend them, but each character comes with their own personality and backstory.

As seen during the Insider Gaming Showcase, the latest Gameplay trailer reveals even more of the expansive world to explore, as well as the unique sandbox events that can occur. The beauty of a game like this is that you have full autonomy to create your character the way you want, with a variety of species and backgrounds to choose from. Also, random events will occur throughout your experience that may not happen in someone else’s. Of course, you will have to farm, craft, and sustain your village, but if you so choose, you can do so with friends either online or through local co-op.

What Is Hawthorn?

To find out more about this upcoming open-world experience, Insider Gaming spoke with Heather Cerlan of NEARstudios. Her background includes credits on The Last of Us, Starfield, and Dragon Age. Check out the full interview below:

Key Features

Make an Impact Outside of Gaming

Early in May, it was announced that players will get the opportunity to reserve three exclusive characters that are all inspired by real-world endangered species. Each character will be designed to fit into the world of the game while maintaining their unique Aesthetics and backgrounds. Additionally, a portion of the proceeds from every reservation will be donated to conservation efforts and Charities that work to protect endangered species and their habitats.

Your Choices Matter

Whether it’s how you choose to solve conflicts, the way you face weather challenges that will occur, or how you interact with the creations around you, your choices matter in this game, and autonomy is key.

Villagers Help You Build

Harvest, fish, craft, and farm. That’s how you survive.  If you don’t want to always get your hands dirty, the NPC villagers can take on tasks you don’t want to do. Build and decorate the homes among the roots of great trees. If you cater to your villagers’ preferences, they will follow you to the end. 

Hawthorn Trailer

The Studio Behind Hawthorn

NEARstudios is a group of game developers from AAA studios like  Bethesda, BioWare, and Naughty Dog who are taking the indie path, developing their first passion project with the help of their players and community. Bethesda, in particular, is known for developing the Fable series, which this game resembles. There’s no release date planned yet, but they’re giving their all to the upcoming experience.

item.title: Project Madras Playtest Announcement Revealed at Insider Gaming Showcase
item.content:encoded:
An open-world game in India isn’t something you see too often, and while the setting is quite unique, the premise is familiar: Infiltrate a gangster network, work as an agent for the government, complete jobs and side hustles, and build your name and brand in a big open world.

Project Madras is ‘a celebration of India’s culture, language, emotions and spirit.’

There’s a real, vibrant, open world here to explore, and it could be a very special one you can get involved with via a new playtest. Full details are below, revealed during the Insider Gaming Showcase.

What Is Project Madras?

Key Features

  • Dual Identity Gameplay:
    • Balance life between criminal networks and covert government objectives.
  • Dynamic Driving & Street Racing:
    • Ride bikes, trucks, and boats across the city, with street races and high-speed escapes.
  • Melee Combat with Stealth & Environmental Takedowns:
    • Close-quarters combat that blends stealth, hand-to-hand fighting, and using surroundings to gain advantage.
  • Side Missions & Street Activities:
    • Optional jobs and gigs that feed progression and world engagement.
  • Semi-Linear Open World Structure:
    • Free exploration with controlled Cinematic Missions within the open world.
  • Authentic World:
    • A dense urban space inspired by Chennai with local details and Crowd behavior.

Project Madras Trailer

The Studio Behind Project Madras

Project Madras is developed by a small independent studio based in India, focused on building grounded, narrative-rich experiences with a strong cultural identity, driven by a unique vision to optimize development through new technology and unconventional first-principles thinking.

Links & More Information

item.title: Waters of Ragnarök Heads Out To Sea At The Insider Gaming Showcase
item.content:encoded:
Waters of Ragnarök is a post-Ragnarök tactical survival game from Polar Region Games, revealed as part of the Insider Gaming Showcase on May 28.

Set in a drowned Norse mythology-inspired world, the game places players in command of a ship built from the bones of dead gods. As you explore the fractured remnants of the Nine Realms, you must manage your crew, harvest resources from fallen Jötnar, and fight to guide your people toward Gimli in an effort to rebuild civilization from the ruins of Ragnarök.

The game blends survival systems, tactical combat, and exploration across a supernatural ocean world filled with monsters, storms, and ancient magic.

What Is Waters of Ragnarök?

To learn more about Waters of Ragnarök and its development, we sat down with the founder and CEO of developer Polar Region Games, Höskuldur Sigurðarson

Key Features

A Drowned Norse World

Explore the flooded remains of the Nine Realms. Players sail between fractured islands, uncover hidden regions, and navigate supernatural storms while discovering what remains of both land and sea.

Deep Origin System

Players choose from distinct origins tied to the Nine Realms. Each origin influences playstyle, crew management, and combat mechanics, adding replayability and strategic depth.

Tactical Combat

Combat focuses on positioning, engagement control, and environmental hazards. Battles are fast-paced and tactical, with success depending more on decision-making than raw stats.

Crew Survival and Morale

Crew members are not just resources. Players must manage hunger, injuries, and morale. Death is permanent, and low morale can cause the expedition to collapse before external threats do.

Jötunn Harvesting

Players can scale and harvest the remains of fallen giants to collect rare materials. These encounters function as both resource-gathering opportunities and large-scale environmental challenges.

Waters of Ragnarök Trailer

The Studio Behind Waters of Ragnarök

Polar Region Games is an independent studio based in Reykjavík, Iceland, focused on building games inspired by Norse mythology.

Founded by a small team of lifelong gamers, the studio operates as a lean development group with full ownership of their IP under the umbrella of their shared universe, Ashes of Yggdrasil. Waters of Ragnarök is their debut title within this world, with additional projects currently in concept development.

Links & More Information

  • Steam Page: https://store.steampowered.com/app/4422960/Waters_of_Ragnark/

item.title: New ZeroSpace Trailer Takes Off at Insider Gaming Showcase
item.content:encoded:
ZeroSpace is a cinematic sci-fi real-time strategy game built around three pillars, featuring a story-rich campaign with RPG-style choices, a persistent cooperative Galactic War, and a competitive Skirmish Mode.

The title combines classic RTS base-building and army control with campaign and part-member structure, inspired by narrative RPGs like Mass Effect and Baldur’s Gate.

ZeroSpace will be available to try in the Steam Next Fest in June, with early access planned to launch in Q3 2026 on Steam.

What is ZeroSpace?

To learn more about ZeroSpace and its development, take a look at our developer interview.

Key Features

Story-Rich Sci-Fi Campaign

A cinematic single-player campaign where player choices, crew relationships, and faction alliances affect the story. The campaign includes main story missions, hero loyalty missions, and side story arcs, with dialogue trees and choice-driven outcomes.

Persistent Galactic War Co-Op Mode

ZeroSpace includes a shared Galactic War mode where players contribute to a larger seasonal conflict across a persistent galaxy map. Mission variety includes co-op, PvE, single-player activities, 2vAI, 2v2, scenarios, and 1v1. The mode is similar to Helldivers 2, but with more factions and an RTS core gameplay mode.

Faction & Army Customization

Players build their strategy by combining main factions, mercenary factions, specialists, heroes, units, and buildings. ZeroSpace will have four main factions, seven mercenary factions, over 14 specialists, and more than 150 units and buildings.

Modernized RTS Controls & Quality-of-Life Features

ZeroSpace is designed to preserve the depth of classic RTS games, while reducing unnecessary friction. Many players have already expressed how it’s hard to go back to other RTS games without these QOL features.

Competitive VS Designed with RTS Veterans

1v1 and team-based versus modes designed with the help of RTS veterans, including prominent StarCraft personalities like CatZ, Scarlett, GiantGrantGames, Maguro, and PiG.

ZeroSpace Trailer

The Studio Behind ZeroSpace

Starlance Studios is a remote, independent studio building ZeroSpace out of a long-running love for classic real-time strategy games like Command and Conquer, StarCraft, and Age of Empires.

ZeroSpace began as a solo development effort by Marv Gouw, before growing into a team of roughly 15 people alongside additional part-time contributors.

Starlance Studios has recently been joined by Aaron Kambeitz, a co-founder of Relic Entertainment and lead artist on the sci-fi classic Homeworld. Under his art direction, ZeroSpace has found its visual soul.

Links & More Information

item.title: Hope in the City Debuts Fresh Trailer With Exciting Showcase Look
item.content:encoded:
Hope in the City is an apt title as our titular Hope Song needs all the prayers in the world as she searches the city in the hope (pun not intended) of finding her parents.

Hope in the City is described as a ‘Non-linear narrative mystery game from the team behind 2026 GDC Best in Play Winning sci-fi fantasy adventure-RPG Sky of Tides.’

The blocky, atmospheric art style complements the sense of despair, and Lofty Sky Games has lofty ambitions for its narrative thriller, which you can learn more about, thanks to the Insider Gaming Showcase.

What Is Hope in the City?

Key Features

  • Solve the Mystery Through Artful Investigation
    • Gather clues, record observations, and piece together the truth using sketches in Hope’s notebook. Dive deep into the investigation to uncover what lies beneath the city’s calm facade.
  • A Unique Gameplay Mechanic, Based on Thoughts
    • Progress by building your observations into insights. Then, make breakthroughs to get closer to solving the mysterious disappearance of your parents.
  • An Art Style with Edge for a Unique Experience
    • Experience a bold art style that blends story and visuals, creating a unique world that invites closer exploration. Experience custom cutscenes throughout the game as you progress in solving the mystery!

Hope in the City Trailer

The Studio Behind Hope in the City

Lofty Sky Games team members have worked on games such as Battlefield, Minecraft Legends, and I Was A Teenage Exocolonist, and worked at studios including EA, Ubisoft, and Behaviour Interactive. 

The animation team is equally impressive, having created numerous award-winning projects, including the first-ever animated feature selected as Canada’s official submission to the Oscars for Best International Film.

item.title: CRETE Gets New Gameplay Trailer at Insider Gaming Showcase
item.content:encoded:
CRETE just received a stunning new gameplay trailer during the Insider Gaming Showcase, unveiling more of this innovative, intriguing roguelike title. Mayhem Mirror Studios is piecing together CRETE, and it’s set to be the firm’s debut title. Everything from the visual palette to the weapons you use is novel in CRETE, and the new trailer reveals more of what’s coming.

CRETE doesn’t have a release window locked in just yet, but it’s firmly a recommendation for a prestigious spot on your Steam wishlist. Check out everything you need to know about CRETE below.

What is CRETE?

CRETE is an intense action-roguelike title that catapults players into a brutal world populated with hulking, terrifying enemies. It’s a battle between good and evil; between god-like entities called the Ancestors and a race of bioengineered warriors that evolve massively over time.

As you explore CRETE, you’ll constantly adapt and mutate, collecting critical DNA samples to alter your body and create fresh ways to fight. In the words of Mayhem Mirror Studios, there are millions of combinations to discover.

Players can either go it alone or fight together, but the team has stressed that CRETE blossoms best when enjoyed cooperatively. It’s an inherently tricky game, but it’s not impossible, and players will find the combat mechanics to be fast-paced, fresh, and energetic.

Key Features

Multi-Faceted Combat Mechanics

CRETE encourages a range of playstyles and approaches, such is the roguelike nature of the game. With that in mind, you can go hand-to-hand, use intense melee weapons, or pick your enemies off from a distance, utilizing the mutation mechanic to keep things mixed as you explore the game.

True Cooperative Gameplay

Mayhem Mirror Studios has advised that CRETE can be completed as a solo player, but for the best experience possible, it’s advised to go at it with a friend. As it states on Steam: ‘The world is waiting to demonstrate the real meaning of teamwork.’

Bioengineering Approach

CRETE uses a unique bioengineering concept to empower the player, ensuring they can evolve in ways they never thought possible. As CRETE unfolds, players will lean on millions of potential mutation combinations, growing as a character and a combatant in this harsh world.

CRETE Developer Interview

Check out our interview with Mayhem Mirror Studios here:

The Studio Behind CRETE

CRETE is being developed by Mayhem Mirror Studios, a two-person team consisting of a game director and developer, and an art director and 3D modeller. Until now, Mayhem Mirror has focused on creating animated media, such as short films, but CRETE will be the team’s first foray into video games.

CRETE has a demo coming this summer, so keep your eyes peeled and your wishlists loaded.

Links and More Information

  • Website: https://www.cretegame.com/
  • Twitter: https://x.com/cretegame
  • Store: https://store.steampowered.com/app/2022610/CRETE/

item.title: New Etre Trailer & Details Revealed at Insider Gaming Showcase
item.content:encoded:
Etre is a 3D third-person action adventure, set in an ancient alien mind spiralling toward collapse, where players explore shifting inner worlds, unlock new abilities, and battle hostile anomalies.

The Metroidvania title is set inside Orbis, a planet-scale alien mind, where players explore a vast, interconnected labyrinth of luminous architecture to solve spatial puzzles and engage in fluid ground and aerial combat.

Inspired by the visionary sci-fi of 70s-80s European graphic novels, the journey has multiple endings, with choices and discoveries shaping whether Orbis stabilizes, becomes a god-mind, or awakens and something far more terrifying.

What is Etre?

To learn more about Etre and its development, take a look at our developer interview.

Key Features

Discover a Beautiful World

Explore a vast, interconnected 3D labyrinth, with abilities that open new paths through familiar spaces.

Fluid Ground & Aerial Combat

Switch between ground and aerial combat, destroying, subduing, or repurposing strange machines and anomalies as tools and allies. Confront boss encounters that play like duels and riddles, demanding both reflexes and understanding.

Save The One You Love

Follow in the footsteps of Atel, your bonded one, and navigate the competing visions of two Etre leaders with incompatible plans for Orbis and Lucent, the structured light that forms this world. Piece together a non-linear story about love, awakening, and responsibility.

Etre Trailer

The Studio Behind Etre

Studio Lifeforms is a game design studio based in Los Angeles, making virtual worlds and experiences using real-time technologies.

It is a two-person independent game studio, with co-founders Damjan Jovanovic and Lidija Kljakovic working alongside each other.

Links & More Information

Steam: https://store.steampowered.com/app/2391940/ETRE/

Website: https://lifeforms.io/

Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/lifeforms.io/

Let us know what you think about Etre through the Insider Gaming Discord server, which is free to join!

Check the rest of our website, as we have news on over 50 titles shown off during the Insider Gaming Showcase!

item.title: Sirius Dice Announces Official Critical Role Vox Machina Dice Set
item.content:encoded:
Critical Role fans have some new sets of dice to collect. Sirius Dice has announced a new line of Critical Role Vox Machina dice, bringing the personality of the actual play series' heroes to every gaming table's most important tool.

According to a press release from Sirius Dice, their Critical Role Vox Machina Dice sets will be complete sets of eight polyhedral dice. A d20, a d12, a d8, 2d10, a d6, and a d4, meaning a single set of dice will have you ready for any adventure you undertake, be it Dungeons & Dragons or Pathfinder. 

There will be seven sets of dice in total, with each one, both the packaging and the dice containing visual inspirations for each member of the adventuring party Vox Machina: Grog Strongjaw, Vex'alia, Vax'ildan, Pike Trickfoot, Scanlan Shorthalt, and Percival de Rolo.

The party of Vox Machina are the most iconic characters of Critical Role. The heroes of Campaign 1 of the actual play series captured the hearts of fans thanks to strong, committed performances by their players, and compelling, ongoing narratives brought on by dice rolls and choice. 

Their adventures have since been adapted into the Prime Video animated series, The Legend of Vox Machina, with its fourth season debuting June 3rd on the platform.

The press materials showcasing the Vox Machina dice continue Sirius' attention to detail. For example, Percival's dice appear metallic with flecks of gold and metal, blending his engineering skill with his royal background. In addition, the highest value on certain dice feature different bits of character iconography: his glasses, a bullet, and the Vox Machina symbol.

This care and craft is apparent in every set. Grog's dice being an earthy blend of red and black sporting tankards, his axe, and the Titanstone Knuckles. Vex and Vax's dice being translucent with different shades of blue denoting them as twins. Scanlan's dice having glitter, representing his love of theatrics and bardic performance. Pike's dice having a bit of a glow since she's a Cleric.

Thankfully, these Critical Role Vox Machina dice won't break the bank either. Each set will be available at an MSRP of $19.99 on Sirius' web store or at certain hobby stores near you. The dice sets will be available on July 2026.

Ever since he was small, Tyler Chancey has had a deep, abiding love for video games and a tendency to think and overanalyze everything he enjoyed. This… More about Tyler

item.title: Paddler Premieres at Insider Gaming Showcase
item.content:encoded:
Paddler is a rhythmic typing adventure where players embody a groovy bartender. The game requires players to watch the bar lanes while fulfilling orders. These orders need to be served by mixing the right ingredients, and to do so, you will have to type out each ingredient individually.

The order of the ingredients doesn’t matter, but what does matter is pressing Enter after each ingredient is typed. Typing everything on one line will not work; for example, you have to type ‘coconut’, press enter, then type ‘lime’, and so on. Once all the ingredients have been entered, drinks will be automatically served.

That’s not all there is to Paddler, as we are yet to cover the rhythmic bit. All your keystrokes and enter presses will be rhythm-tracked, and if you are on the beat, you will build a combo and earn more tips. If you are off beat, it will result in a lower score. Going too offbeat will break your groove.

Check out all the drinks that are currently available in the game below:

  • Bahama Mama
  • Hurricane
  • Kiwi Kiss
  • Tequila Sunrise
  • Coco Cooler
  • Sunset Spritz
  • Blue Lagoon
  • Daiquiri
  • Mai Tai
  • Mojito
  • Piña Colada

Paddler Trailer

What is Paddler?

The Insider Gaming Showcase is finally here, bringing players world premieres, some really underrated indie games, and lots more. Here’s a tracker for everything announced at the event, with some real gems hidden in the mix. Paddler is one of these indie titles, which tests your bartending and typing skills. You can watch the full interview on Paddler and learn more below:

The Studio Behind Paddler

Paddler comes from the hands of Kosma Games, whose founder, Zachary Kosma, was able to develop this game in 15 hours with the help of Unity, Bezi, ChatGPT + DALLE, ChatGPT Codex, Gimp, JetBrains Rider, Github Co-Pilot, and Pixabay for the music.

Links and More

Link: https://keyboarmon.com/paddler.html

Check out Insider Gaming’s Steam Curator. What are your thoughts on Paddler? Leave your thoughts down in the comments, and join the official Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more information from Insider Gaming, read about Forza Horizon 6 mods already surfacing online. Don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

item.title: Good News Release Date Revealed During Insider Gaming Showcase
item.content:encoded:
Good News is an all-new indie title from a solo developer, and it just received a release date reveal during the Insider Gaming Showcase. This game is deeply appropriate for us as journalists, as it tells the tale of a chief editor hired to help a failing newspaper grow.

It’s a charming simulation game with a branching, intelligent narrative that encourages you to manage your team to get the most out of them, and to avoid having your newspaper go under. Below, check out the trailer, our interview with Augusto Salgado, the game’s developer, and more.

What is Good News?

Good news is the opposite of bad news! Sorry, that’s an awful joke.

Good News is a brand-new indie title from Augusto Salgado, a dedicated solo developer, and it throws players into the role of chief editor at The Citizen, a failing newspaper that is in sore need of a revival. As the chief editor, you’ll decide which news hits the front page, how to grow your audience, and which relationships you want to foster or turn your back on.

It’s a game with multiple endings, and a narrative that ebbs and flows as the story unravels. You’re the master of your own corner of the journalism empire, but will you fly or fall?

Key Features

Charming Retro Visuals

Good News boasts a delightful retro palette that’s simple and reminds you of the bygone days of traditional print media. It’s a low-stimulation title that can be played at your own pace, and the basic visuals are a testament to that concept.

Branching Narrative

Every newspaper holds a stack of stories, and so does Good News. In this game, you’ll explore a narrative that evolves as you play, and the decisions you make and the stories you publish will determine exactly how your adventure unfolds.

Good News Developer Interview

Check out our interview with Augusto Salgado:

The Developer Behind Good News

Good News is Augusto Salgado’s first game, which he has been assembling from his home in Argentina. He’s piecing the game together and has eyed up a PC launch. Previously, Salgado worked as a game programmer.

Links and More Information

  • Website: https://elephteria.ar/
  • Discord: https://discord.com/invite/kcHA99Vwkh
  • Store: https://store.steampowered.com/app/3069820/Good_News/

Does Good News look like your kind of game? Let us know on the Insider Gaming Discord server

Senior Editor and Esports Lead

Grant has been gaming for 30+ years and in the industry for 10+. You'll probably find him playing a post-apocalyptic game or an extraction shooter somewhere.

More from Grant Taylor-Hill

item.title: Zoominoes Unveils New Roguelike Gameplay Trailer At Insider Gaming Showcase
item.content:encoded:
Zoominoes is a unique take on the popular roguelike deckbuilder genre. Build the ultimate zoo as you watch your numbers go up and up as you break the game with insane tile combos. 

If you’re lucky, you’ll pull powerful mythical animals that can be boosted with snacks to rack up even higher scores. With hundreds of different tiles to collect and use, the combos that you can pull off are truly endless. 

With an addictive free demo already available on Steam, Zoominoes is bounding towards its full release later this Summer, with mobile support planned for post-launch. 

What Is Zoominoes? 

To learn more about Zoominoes and its development, we sat down with developer Matthew Nass from Starlight Games.

Key features

Tile-Based Gameplay

Zoominoes revolves around placing different tiles from your deck onto a Scrabble-like board. Each tile can be combined in a variety of unique ways to manipulate the board and score massive points. 

Deckbuilding Delight

You’ll be able to use snacks and souvenirs to create crazy combos, all whilst transforming your starting deck into your perfect masterpiece. With different animal types, colors, and zookeepers to work around, there are tons of strategies to discover.

Endless Content

There’s an insane amount of content coming to Zoominoes. At launch, there will be: 

  • 150+ Animal Tiles
  • 100+ Souvenirs
  • 20+ Zookeepers

As well as challenge levels, difficulty settings and daily challenges, all available from day one.

Zoominoes Trailer

The Studio Behind Zoominoes

Zoominoes is being developed by Starlight Games, and it was founded by Magic: The Gathering veterans Josh Utter-Leyton and Matthew Nass. With a solid team to their name, the studio is set on developing strategy games, and with such a strong history in the deckbuilding genre, it seems like they are well-suited to the job. 

Links & More Information

  • Website
  • Discord
  • Steam

What do you make of Zoominoes? If it piques your interest, be sure to add it to your Steam wishlist and share your thoughts in the official Insider Gaming Discord Server!

Charlie is one of Insider Gaming's Guides Writers who's been in the gaming industry for a little over a year. Unfortunately for him, he enjoys every video game genre out…

item.title: Hexrite Gets Release Window at Insider Gaming Showcase
item.content:encoded:
Hexrite is a game in which players work toward completing a magical ritual that summons an ancient deity. The game will have you dealing with gods, using all types of magic, a variety of spells, and exploring various cities where you can try out different platforming and traversal abilities.

Regarding the magic in Hexrite, players will use different types of it to deal with various enemies, including feats such as summoning paper constructs, conjuring poison clouds, and turning the floor into a field of bubbles.

In your quest to summon a deity, you will meet various others as well. Some of them will help you out, while others might need a sacrifice in order to acquire more power. Players should note that their fate depends on the relationships they make along the way.

There are a variety of spells in your arsenal that you will use against enemies and bosses through a classic action-packed combat system. These spells can be upgraded to discover new uses, while players can also discover forbidden magic for an additional edge in battles.

Hexrite will have players explore haunted cities and floating towers, where they will discover various platforming and movement abilities that let them perform stunts like bouncing on top of jellyfish and gliding across flaming peaks. The game is set to arrive in late 2026.

Hexrite Trailer

Key Features

  • DOMES OF MAGIC – Summon powerful fields of magic that change the battlefield surrounding you and your enemies: summon paper constructs, conjure up poison clouds, or turn the floor into a slippery field of bubbles!
  • A DEAL WITH THE GODS – Meet shadowy deities throughout your adventure that will either aid you, or demand a sacrifice in return for power, but be careful, the relationships you choose might alter your fate..
  • SPELLS GALORE – Access a vast arsenal of spells that you can use against enemies and bosses in a classic action-packed combat system, upgrade spells for completely new uses, and uncover forbidden magic to help you defeat your foes!
  • MAGICAL LANDS – Explore haunted cities and floating towers using various platforming movement abilities, from bouncing on top of jellyfish to gliding across fiery peaks!

What is Hexrite?

The Insider Gaming Showcase has finally arrived, and it has something for everyone, featuring indie games across most genres. Interested readers can check out our Everything Announced piece to learn about all the games that were a part of the event. To learn more about Hexrite, check out Insider Gaming’s interview with the developer below:

Studio Behind Hexrite

Phabula is an indie game company focused on delivering stylized titles that blend fun, engaging gameplay mechanics inspired by classic games with a modern touch.

Links and More

  • Steam Page: https://store.steampowered.com/app/2621770/Hexrite/
  • Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/phabulagames/

Check out Insider Gaming’s Steam Curator. What are your thoughts on Hexrite? Leave your thoughts down in the comments, and join the official Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more information from Insider Gaming, read about Images of a new Xbox controller for cloud gaming leaking. Don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

item.title: UNGALAND’s Showcases Pre-Historic World During the Insider Gaming Showcase
item.content:encoded:
UNGALAND is a unique independent video game project. It’s an action-adventure platformer set in an original take on prehistoric times. A new trailer was recently featured at the Insider Gaming Showcase, offering a unique look at the interactive environment and challenging enemies that the game aims to bring to life. 

Players will control Oogie, a member of the Bunga tribe, and fulfill their assigned mission. Overcome every challenge the island has to offer and return victorious, not as the hero they expect, but as the brute they need. Along the way, players will be collecting primal seeds in an effort to gain new abilities that will help them along the way. 

Mevstudio plans to crowdfund for the game via a Kickstarter campaign. The hope is that there will be sufficient funding to establish the core gameplay, the central campaign, and the world of UNGALAND. The team hopes to expand from there. Insider Gaming will continue to monitor the development of this upcoming title and report updates as the game nears completion.

What Is UNGALAND?

To learn more about this forthcoming game, Insider Gaming spoke with Mevstudio’s Esteban Verdezoto, the co-director of the platformer, about the project and what they hope to accomplish.

Key Features

Among the standout features of this project are an attractive and unique art style, dynamic combat, exploration, challenging boss fights, accessible controls, and more.

UNGALAND Trailer

The Studio Behind UNGALAND

Mevstudio is a small, dedicated group of artists from around the world. They’re United by their love of creativity, storytelling, and obviously, video games. UNGALAND is a project that looks to bring their unique artistic Vision to life and highlight the skills of each individual member.

Links & More Information

  • Website: https://www.ungaland.com
  • Twitter: https://x.com/Ungaland_game
  • Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/unga.land/
  • Kickstarter: https://www.kickstarter.com/projects/ungaland/ungaland
  • Store: https://store.steampowered.com/app/3367020/UNGALAND/
